heading: Reserves for losses on loans

Prior to such amendment, section read as follows:Pub. L. 87–834“§ 593. Additions to reserve for bad debts“In the case of a mutual savings bank not having capital stock represented by shares, a domestic building and loan association, and a cooperative bank without capital stock organized and operated for mutual purposes and without profit, the reasonable addition to a reserve for bad debts under section 166(c) shall be determined with due regard to the amount of the taxpayer’s surplus or bad debt reserves existing at the close of . In the case of a taxpayer described in the preceding sentence, the reasonable addition to a reserve for bad debts for any taxable year shall in no case be less than the amount determined by the taxpayer as the reasonable addition for such year; except that the amount determined by the taxpayer under this sentence shall not be greater than the lesser of—December 31, 1951“(1) the amount of its taxable income for the taxable year, computed without regard to this section, or“(2) the amount by which 12 percent of the total deposits or withdrawable accounts of its depositors at the close of such year exceeds the sum of its surplus, undivided profits, and reserves at the beginning of the taxable year.”

(b)(2). , substituted a table of applicable percentages of the taxable income for each year up to 1979 and thereafter for the amount in excess of 60 percent over the amount referred to in former subsec. (b)(1)(A), transferred the remaining provisions of former subsec. (b)(2) to subpart (D), and added subpars. (B) to (E).Pub. L. 91–172, § 432(a)(2)Subsec. Prior to such amendment, section read as follows:Pub. L. 87–834“§ 593. Additions to reserve for bad debts“In the case of a mutual savings bank not having capital stock represented by shares, a domestic building and loan association, and a cooperative bank without capital stock organized and operated for mutual purposes and without profit, the reasonable addition to a reserve for bad debts under section 166(c) shall be determined with due regard to the amount of the taxpayer’s surplus or bad debt reserves existing at the close of . In the case of a taxpayer described in the preceding sentence, the reasonable addition to a reserve for bad debts for any taxable year shall in no case be less than the amount determined by the taxpayer as the reasonable addition for such year; except that the amount determined by the taxpayer under this sentence shall not be greater than the lesser of—December 31, 1951“(1) the amount of its taxable income for the taxable year, computed without regard to this section, or“(2) the amount by which 12 percent of the total deposits or withdrawable accounts of its depositors at the close of such year exceeds the sum of its surplus, undivided profits, and reserves at the beginning of the taxable year.”

children.label: A
children.text: any domestic building and loan association,

children.label: B
children.text: any mutual savings bank, or

children.label: C
children.text: any cooperative bank without capital stock organized and operated for mutual purposes and without profit,

children.heading: Organization must meet 60-percent asset test of section 7701(a)(19)
children.label: 2 Organization must meet 60-percent asset test of section 7701(a)(19)
children.text: This section shall apply to an association or bank referred to in paragraph (1) only if it meets the requirements of section 7701(a)(19)(C).

children.heading: Addition to reserves for bad debts
children.label: b Addition to reserves for bad debts
A
children.text: the amount determined to be a reasonable addition to the reserve for losses on nonqualifying loans, computed in the same manner as is provided with respect to additions to the reserves for losses on loans of banks under section 585(b)(2), plus

children.label: i
children.text: the amount determined under paragraph (3), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount which, when added to the amount determined under subparagraph (A), equals the amount by which 12 percent of the total deposits or withdrawable accounts of depositors of the taxpayer at the close of such year exceeds the sum of its surplus, undivided profits, and reserves at the beginning of such year (taking into account any portion thereof attributable to the period before the first taxable year beginning after ).December 31, 1951

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), the amount determined under this paragraph for the taxable year shall be an amount equal to 8 percent of the taxable income for such year.

children.heading: Reduction for amounts referred to in paragraph (1)(A)
children.label: B Reduction for amounts referred to in paragraph (1)(A)
children.text: The amount determined under subparagraph (A) shall be reduced (but not below 0) by the amount determined under paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Overall limitation on paragraph
children.label: C Overall limitation on paragraph
children.text: The amount determined under this paragraph shall not exceed the amount necessary to increase the balance at the close of the taxable year of the reserve for losses on qualifying real property loans to 6 percent of such loans outstanding at such time.

children.label: i
children.text: by excluding from gross income any amount included therein by reason of subsection (e),

children.label: ii
children.text: without regard to any deduction allowable for any addition to the reserve for bad debts,

children.label: iii
children.text: by excluding from gross income an amount equal to the net gain for the taxable year arising from the sale or exchange of stock of a corporation or of obligations the interest on which is excludable from gross income under section 103,

children.label: iv
children.text: by excluding from gross income dividends with respect to which a deduction is allowable by part VIII of subchapter B, reduced by an amount equal to 8 percent of the dividends received deduction for the taxable year, and

children.label: I
children.text: the net long-term capital gain for the taxable year, or

children.label: II
children.text: the net long-term capital gain for the taxable year from the sale or exchange of property other than property described in clause (iii).

children.heading: Experience method
children.label: 3 Experience method
children.text: The amount determined under this paragraph for the taxable year shall be computed in the same manner as is provided with respect to additions to the reserves for losses on loans of banks under section 585(b)(2).

children.heading: Treatment of reserves for bad debts
children.label: c Treatment of reserves for bad debts

children.heading: Establishment of reserves
children.label: 1 Establishment of reserves
children.text: Each taxpayer described in subsection (a) which uses the reserve method of accounting for bad debts shall establish and maintain a reserve for losses on qualifying real property loans, a reserve for losses on nonqualifying loans, and a supplemental reserve for losses on loans. For purposes of this title, such reserves shall be treated as reserves for bad debts, but no deduction shall be allowed for any addition to the supplemental reserve for losses on loans.

children.heading: Certain pre-1963 reserves
children.label: 2 Certain pre-1963 reserves
children.text: Notwithstanding the second sentence of paragraph (1), any amount allocated pursuant to paragraph (5) (as in effect immediately before the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1976) during a taxable year beginning before , to the reserve for losses on qualifying real property loans out of the surplus, undivided profits, and bad debt reserves (determined as of ) attributable to the period before the first taxable year beginning after , shall not be treated as a reserve for bad debts for any purpose other than determining the amount referred to in subsection (b)(1)(B), and for such purpose such amount shall be treated as remaining in such reserve.January 1, 1977December 31, 1962December 31, 1951

children.heading: Charging of bad debts to reserves
children.label: 3 Charging of bad debts to reserves
children.text: Any debt becoming worthless or partially worthless in respect of a qualifying real property loan shall be charged to the reserve for losses on such loans, and any debt becoming worthless or partially worthless in respect of a nonqualifying loan shall be charged to the reserve for losses on nonqualifying loans; except that any such debt may, at the election of the taxpayer, be charged in whole or in part to the supplemental reserve for losses on loans.

children.label: A
children.text: any loan evidenced by a security (as defined in section 165(g)(2)(C));

children.label: i
children.text: a government or political subdivision or instrumentality thereof;

children.label: ii
children.text: a bank (as defined in section 581); or

children.label: iii
children.text: another member of the same affiliated group;

children.label: C
children.text: any loan, to the extent secured by a deposit in or share of the taxpayer; or

children.label: D
children.text: any loan which, within a 60-day period beginning in one taxable year of the creditor and ending in its next taxable year, is made or acquired and then repaid or disposed of, unless the transactions by which such loan was made or acquired and then repaid or disposed of are established to be for bona fide business purposes. For purposes of subparagraph (B)(iii), the term “affiliated group” has the meaning assigned to such term by section 1504(a); except that (i) the phrase “more than 50 percent” shall be substituted for the phrase “at least 80 percent” each place it appears in section 1504(a), and (ii) all corporations shall be treated as includible corporations (without any exclusion under section 1504(b)).

children.heading: Nonqualifying loans
children.label: 2 Nonqualifying loans
children.text: The term “nonqualifying loan” means any loan which is not a qualifying real property loan.

children.heading: Loan
children.label: 3 Loan
children.text: The term “loan” means debt, as the term “debt” is used in section 166.

children.heading: Treatment of interests in REMIC’s
children.label: 4 Treatment of interests in REMIC’s
children.text: A regular or residual interest in a REMIC shall be treated as a qualifying real property loan; except that, if less than 95 percent of the assets of such REMIC are qualifying real property loans (determined as if the taxpayer held the assets of the REMIC), such interest shall be so treated only in the proportion which the assets of such REMIC consist of such loans. For purposes of determining whether any interest in a REMIC qualifies under the preceding sentence, any interest in another REMIC held by such REMIC shall be treated as a qualifying real property loan under principles similar to the principles of the preceding sentence, except that if such REMIC’s are part of a tiered structure, they shall be treated as 1 REMIC for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: first out of its earnings and profits accumulated in taxable years beginning after , (and, in the case of an S corporation, the accumulated adjustments account, as defined in section 1368(e)(1)) to the extent thereof,December 31, 1951

children.label: B
children.text: then out of the balance taken into account under subsection (g)(2)(A)(ii) (properly adjusted for amounts charged against such reserves for taxable years beginning after ),December 31, 1987

children.label: C
children.text: then out of the supplemental reserve for losses on loans, to the extent thereof,

children.label: D
children.text: then out of such other accounts as may be proper.

children.heading: Amounts charged to reserve accounts and included in gross income
children.label: 2 Amounts charged to reserve accounts and included in gross income
children.text: If any distribution is treated under paragraph (1) as having been made out of the reserves described in subparagraphs (B) and (C) of such paragraph, the amount charged against such reserve shall be the amount which, when reduced by the amount of tax imposed under this chapter and attributable to the inclusion of such amount in gross income, is equal to the amount of such distribution; and the amount so charged against such reserve shall be included in gross income of the taxpayer.

children.label: A
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(B), additions to the reserve for losses on qualifying real property loans for the taxable year in which the distribution occurs shall be taken into account.

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of computing under this section the amount of a reasonable addition to the reserve for losses on qualifying real property loans for any taxable year, any amount charged during any year to such reserve pursuant to the provisions of paragraph (2) shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Termination of reserve method
children.label: f Termination of reserve method
children.text: Subsections (a), (b), (c), and (d) shall not apply to any taxable year beginning after .December 31, 1995

children.label: A
children.text: such change shall be treated as a change in a method of accounting,

children.label: B
children.text: such change shall be treated as initiated by the taxpayer and as having been made with the consent of the Secretary, and

children.label: i
children.text: shall be determined by taking into account only applicable excess reserves, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as so determined, shall be taken into account ratably over the 6-taxable year period beginning with the first taxable year beginning after .December 31, 1995

children.label: i
children.text: the balance of the reserves described in subsection (c)(1) (other than the supplemental reserve) as of the close of the taxpayer’s last taxable year beginning before , overJanuary 1, 1996

children.label: I
children.text: the balance of such reserves as of the close of the taxpayer’s last taxable year beginning before , orJanuary 1, 1988

children.label: II
children.text: the balance of the reserves described in subclause (I), reduced in the same manner as under section 585(b)(2)(B)(ii) on the basis of the taxable years described in clause (i) and this clause.

children.heading: Special rule for thrifts which become small banks
children.label: B Special rule for thrifts which become small banks
i
children.text: the balance taken into account under subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not be less than the amount which would be the balance of such reserves as of the close of its last taxable year beginning before such date if the additions to such reserves for all taxable years had been determined under section 585(b)(2)(A), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the opening balance of the reserve for bad debts as of the beginning of such first taxable year shall be the balance taken into account under subparagraph (A)(ii) (determined after the application of clause (i) of this subparagraph).

children.heading: Recapture of pre-1988 reserves where taxpayer ceases to be bank
children.label: 3 Recapture of pre-1988 reserves where taxpayer ceases to be bank
children.text: If, during any taxable year beginning after , a taxpayer to which paragraph (1) applied is not a bank (as defined in section 581), paragraph (1) shall apply to the reserves described in paragraph (2)(A)(ii) and the supplemental reserve; except that such reserves shall be taken into account ratably over the 6-taxable year period beginning with such taxable year.December 31, 1995

children.heading: Suspension of recapture if residential loan requirement met
children.label: 4 Suspension of recapture if residential loan requirement met
i
children.text: no adjustment shall be taken into account under paragraph (1) for such taxable year, and

children.label: I
children.text: whether any other taxable year is a taxable year for which an adjustment is required to be taken into account under paragraph (1), and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of such adjustment.

children.heading: Residential loan requirement
children.label: B Residential loan requirement
children.text: A taxpayer meets the residential loan requirement of this subparagraph for any taxable year if the principal amount of the residential loans made by the taxpayer during such year is not less than the base amount for such year.

children.heading: Residential loan
children.label: C Residential loan
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “residential loan” means any loan described in clause (v) of section 7701(a)(19)(C) but only if such loan is incurred in acquiring, constructing, or improving the property described in such clause.

children.heading: Base amount
children.label: D Base amount
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), the base amount is the average of the principal amounts of the residential loans made by the taxpayer during the 6 most recent taxable years beginning on or before . At the election of the taxpayer who made such loans during each of such 6 taxable years, the preceding sentence shall be applied without regard to the taxable year in which such principal amount was the highest and the taxable year in such principal amount was the lowest. Such an election may be made only for the first taxable year beginning after such date, and, if made for such taxable year, shall apply to the succeeding taxable year unless revoked with the consent of the Secretary.December 31, 1995

children.heading: Controlled groups
children.label: E Controlled groups
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer which is a member of any controlled group of corporations described in section 1563(a)(1), subparagraph (B) shall be applied with respect to such group.

children.heading: Continued application of fresh start under section 585 transitional rules
children.label: 5 Continued application of fresh start under section 585 transitional rules

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of determining the net amount of adjustments referred to in section 585(c)(3)(A)(iii), there shall be taken into account only the excess (if any) of the reserve for bad debts as of the close of the last taxable year before the disqualification year over the balance taken into account by such taxpayer under paragraph (2)(A)(ii) of this subsection.

children.heading: Treatment under elective cut-off method
children.label: B Treatment under elective cut-off method
i
children.text: the balance of the reserve taken into account under subparagraph (B) thereof shall be reduced by the balance taken into account by such taxpayer under paragraph (2)(A)(ii) of this subsection, and

children.label: ii
children.text: no amount shall be includible in gross income by reason of such reduction.

children.heading: Suspended reserve included as section 381(c) items
children.label: 6 Suspended reserve included as section 381(c) items
children.text: The balance taken into account by a taxpayer under paragraph (2)(A)(ii) of this subsection and the supplemental reserve shall be treated as items described in section 381(c).

children.label: A
children.text: any amount required to be included in the gross income of the credit union by reason of this subsection shall be treated as derived from an unrelated trade or business (as defined in section 513), and

children.label: B
children.text: for purposes of paragraph (3), the credit union shall not be treated as if it were a bank.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 8 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out this subsection and subsection (e), including regulations providing for the application of such subsections in the case of acquisitions, mergers, spin-offs, and other reorganizations.
